title: Regime experts with beta calibration on OOF scores
summary: Sources on calibration suggest flexible post-hoc mapping can improve probability quality, and F1 strategy is clearly regime-dependent: dry mandatory-compound logic, wet/intermediate behavior, and late-race no-stop zones differ materially.
rationale: Sources on calibration suggest flexible post-hoc mapping can improve probability quality, and F1 strategy is clearly regime-dependent: dry mandatory-compound logic, wet/intermediate behavior, and late-race no-stop zones differ materially. A single global model can blur those regimes.
implementation_hint: Train specialist models for regimes such as dry vs wet/intermediate, early/mid vs late race, or first-stop vs later-stop situations; blend their OOF predictions through a simple meta-model, then apply held-out beta calibration to the final score.
expected_effect: Should sharpen probabilities in heterogeneous regions and make blends safer than raw averaging.
risk: More moving parts; if regime definitions are weak or calibration is not strictly out-of-fold, leakage can erase gains.
